title: Living in Boulder vlog 6 - 15 - 2017: Topography of Rome

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog from Boulder giving casual life updates — helping his mom, going on a ride, being behind on his site. The bulk of the video covers his current reading project: working through a stack of classical reference works on ancient Rome (Platner & Ashby's Topographical Dictionary, Mommsen, and Gibbon) to fuel a Google Earth reconstruction of the city, noting that only ~10% of the Forma Urbis Romae marble plan survives, leaving gaps he wants to fill with the dictionary.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: wants to make it as realistic as possible by using classical sources to fill gaps left by the incomplete Forma Urbis Romae
acknowledges slow progress because the big reference volumes take days per entry
wants to get back to the Google Earth project after finishing the Topographical Dictionary

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's 1:05 AM in 2016, and he's reflecting on 2015.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He worked late yesterday and went on a ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today has been busy, so he hasn't made much progress on his site.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He helped his mom with vacuuming today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was reading Clement of Alexandria last time but has since moved on to other books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He's currently reading 'A Topographical Dictionary of Ancient Rome' from 1920.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He's focusing on classics from the Roman Empire era.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has other books like one from 1780 and another 18,000-page one from the 1860s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He's using the 1920 topography book to fill gaps in his Google Earth project of Rome.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Only 10% of the original topography of Rome is left, leaving big gaps on maps.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to make his Google Earth project as realistic as possible by filling in those gaps.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He's also working on a project in Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He's trying to sell his car to get money for other projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to make a video about selling his car.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His process involves working on big projects that take a long time, like three days for one task.
